summary: DeFi lending is a ~$40-54B deposit category (DefiLlama lending category, April 2026) dominated by Aave, which holds roughly 60-63% market share and a ~$13B+ TVL lead over its nearest rival. The structure has bifurcated: Aave remains the broad-market, multi-chain incumbent (pooled model), while the fastest growth is in modular/isolated architectures led by Morpho (Morpho Blue primitive + curated Vaults), with Euler V2 staging a post-hack comeback. Spark, the lending/yield arm of Sky (formerly MakerDAO), is the #2-#3 player on the back of Sky's stablecoin reserves. Compound has stagnated as a legacy pooled lender, JustLend dominates Tron, and Kamino leads Solana lending. Figures vary by snapshot date; the most cited April-May 2026 readings are reported below.

leaders.name: Aave (V3)
leaders.chain: Ethereum + 14-21 EVM chains (Arbitrum, Base, Polygon, Optimism, Avalanche, Gnosis, BNB, etc.)
leaders.value: ~$17-19.4B TVL; ~60-63% lending market share; >$1T cumulative loan volume
leaders.note: Clear category leader. Pooled (monolithic) money market. V3 is ~96% of Aave TVL; V2 in runoff. Lead over #2 has widened, not narrowed, through the 2025-26 recovery. Reached a record ~$41B+ peak TVL earlier.

leaders.name: Spark (SparkLend / Sky)
leaders.value: SparkLend ~$3.6B; total Spark (incl. Savings + Liquidity Layer) ~$6.8-8B
leaders.note: Lending/yield arm of Sky (formerly MakerDAO). Borrows from Sky's $6.5B+ stablecoin reserves; DAI/USDS money market. Grew TVL during the April 2026 KelpDAO exploit period as capital sought safe harbors.

leaders.name: Morpho (Blue + Vaults)
leaders.value: ~$4.9-7.4B TVL; ~$10B+ total deposits at Nov 2025 peak
leaders.note: Most credible structural challenger and the face of the modular shift. Morpho Blue is a ~650-line immutable primitive for isolated markets; Vaults add a curator allocation layer with tighter spreads. Rose from ~$0.6B (Jan 2024) to multi-billion; raised $175M from a16z/Paradigm. V2 targets $20B+ with intent solvers and cross-chain.

leaders.name: Compound (V3 'Comet')
leaders.note: Original first-mover (kicked off 'DeFi summer' yield farming with COMP in 2020) but stagnated. Founder Robert Leshner shifted focus to Superstate in 2023; under-invested in multi-chain vs Aave. Legacy pooled model; ~6-year security track record cited as its main edge.

leaders.name: JustLend (DAO)
leaders.note: Dominant lender on Tron, tied to Justin Sun ecosystem (USDD). Pooled algorithmic money market. Active loans ~$200M+; JST buyback-and-burn program funded by protocol revenue/USDD rewards.

leaders.name: Kamino Finance
leaders.note: Largest DeFi protocol on Solana and its leading lender. Rebranded Dec 2025 with RWA/institutional products (fixed-rate loans, private credit vault, RWA DEX); Feb 2026 institutional tri-party custody with Anchorage Digital.

leaders.name: Euler (V2)
leaders.note: Comeback story after the ~$200M March 2023 hack. V2 (Sept 2024) is a modular credit layer of ERC-4626 vaults (EVK/EVC) for building isolated, configurable lending markets. TVL rose ~38x in 3 months / +500% post-V2; new ATHs in TVL and borrows.

leaders.name: Sky (formerly MakerDAO)
leaders.note: Pioneer of on-chain lending (overcollateralized DAI, 2018). Rebranded MakerDAO -> Sky and externalized active lending into Spark. Now functions more as the stablecoin/collateral backbone than a direct user-facing money market.

leadership_shift: 2018-2020: MakerDAO pioneered on-chain lending with overcollateralized DAI; Compound then ignited 'DeFi summer' (2020) with the COMP liquidity-mining model and briefly led. 2021: A multi-protocol top tier - by Oct 2021 Aave (~$18.9B) had overtaken Compound (~$12B), with MakerDAO (~$16.4B) also huge; Aave won via faster innovation (flash loans, more assets) and aggressive multi-chain/TradFi expansion. 2023: Aave consolidated the #1 spot with V3 and broad multi-chain deployment while Compound stagnated (founder pivoted to Superstate) and Euler suffered a ~$200M hack; MakerDAO spun out SparkLend. 2024-2026: The category split into two tracks - (1) Aave entrenched as the pooled, multi-chain incumbent (~60-63% share, ~$13B+ lead) with Spark as the Sky-backed #2/#3; and (2) a modular/isolated-market wave led by Morpho (Blue primitive + curated Vaults, ~$10B deposits at peak) and a recovering Euler V2. The narrative is specialization rather than displacement: Morpho/Euler grew by pulling capital off monolithic pools with isolated, curator-driven markets and tighter spreads, while Aave defended its liquidity moat. Chain-specific leaders emerged too: JustLend on Tron and Kamino on Solana (~75% of Solana lending).
